docs.text: Patients presenting with chest pain suggestive of acute coronary syndrome should receive an ECG within 10 minutes of arrival and troponin testing per institutional protocol. Risk stratification follows the HEART score; admit pathways are defined for high-risk strata.
STEMI activation requires ST-segment elevation ≥1 mm in two contiguous leads (≥2 mm in V2–V3) on ECG. Door-to-balloon time goal is 90 minutes for percutaneous coronary intervention.

matches.label: Pulmonary embolism without acute cor pulmonale

output.red_flag: Chest pain with radiation to left arm in an adult with cardiovascular risk factors — possible acute coronary syndrome; recommend emergent ECG within 10 minutes and troponin per ACC/AHA ACS 2026 §3.1.

differentials.label: Dissection of aorta
argues_against: pain quality is crushing, not tearing

next_steps: If STEMI criteria met → activate cath lab; door-to-balloon goal 90 min (ACC/AHA ACS 2026 §3.2).
